title: ptilagrostis subsessiliflora
intro:
Ptilagrostis subsessiliflora, also known as Needle-and-Thread Grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its unique, thread-like spikelets and needle-like leaves.

The plant is believed to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations.

Research on Ptilagrostis subsessiliflora suggests that it may offer several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and potential antimicrobial properties.

In traditional herbal medicine, Ptilagrostis subsessiliflora is often prepared as a tea or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
